title: Annotation pipeline: plate-solve + catalog cross-match + annotation model JSON
description: Phase 1 of the labeled-image feature. New optional subpackage uranometria.annotate behind an [annotate] extra (astropy + astroquery; core stays pyyaml-only). Components: ASTAP CLI wrapper (offline solver, scale hints for Seestar S50 ~2.4"/px native / ~1.2"/px drizzled), field cross-match (DSOs from the bundled OpenNGC/Sh2 catalog filtered by separation from solved center; field stars via VizieR Tycho-2 cone query with SIMBAD names for bright stars, gated behind allow_online; ASTAP local DB as offline fallback), and a JSON annotation model sidecar (WCS, per-object pixel+sky coords, designation, mag, class, distance, provenance). CLI: uranometria annotate <image> with --mag-limit (default 11.5, cap ~15 labeled stars) and --offline. Solve the star-rich stack, never the starless finished render. First target: M51 stack — must identify the bright left-edge star (HD 117815, V=7.08) plus NGC 5195 and field NGC/IC. Reviewer pack: extend network-boundary sanctioned endpoints to VizieR/SIMBAD (gated).
close_reason: Implemented on branch annotate-pipeline (stacked on PR #1). Verified end-to-end on the M51 1411-sub stack: solve matches reference, HD 117815 + 11 DSOs + 15 field stars with correct magnitudes/distances. PNG/HTML renderers are uranometria-2/3/4.

title: Annotated PNG renderer with legend panel
description: Phase 2. Render the annotation model onto the image as a static PNG: colored markers/callouts by object class, star circles with numbered keys, side legend panel (DSO list + field-star table with designation/mag/distance), title bar with solve info, N/E compass, scale bar. matplotlib WCSAxes or Pillow. Output Images/<obj>/finished/<obj>_annotated.png style. Match the visual language of the M51_1411_annotated_legend.png reference.

title: Standalone interactive HTML page for a single annotated image
description: Phase 3. uranometria annotate --html: one self-contained page for a single image — the photo inside an SVG with overlay markers/labels in image pixel coords, reusing the chart page's pan/zoom (counter-scaled markers), sidebar legend with search, and design language. Each object links out: SIMBAD by identifier always; Wikipedia when a common name/Messier makes an article likely. LABELS on/off toggle in the display. External links are navigations, allowed under the self-contained rule; the image may be embedded as a data URI for a truly single file.

title: Sky-map lightbox: annotation overlay with labels toggle
description: Phase 4. When a chart object's image has an annotation model sidecar (auto-discovered <image>.annotations.json or explicit annotations: key in the config), the sky-map lightbox renders the overlay on the displayed photo: SVG markers/labels scaled to the displayed image size, plus a LABELS on/off toggle in the lightbox chrome (default off, remembers choice per session). Degrades to the plain photo when no sidecar exists. Keep the lightbox self-contained: overlay data rides in the page, no fetches.

title: Lightbox: scroll-zoom and pan on the image; only click-outside dismisses
description: Current behavior: clicking anywhere (on or off the photo) closes the lightbox, and the image shows the zoom-out cursor on hover (the whole .lightbox has cursor:zoom-out and a single click handler). Wanted: interacting with the image must not dismiss it. Preferred design: give the lightbox image the same interaction model as the sky chart discs (wheel zooms about the cursor, drag pans, double-click resets, counter-scaled feel); clicking the backdrop OUTSIDE the image (or Esc) closes. Fix the cursor affordances to match (grab/grabbing on the image, zoom-out only on the backdrop). Applies to page.py's lightbox markup/CSS/JS; also carries into the uranometria-4 annotation-overlay lightbox, which should share this interaction.

title: Example config: color-code objects by type; Orion colored as nebula
description: In examples/skymap.yaml, assign per-object color: values so classes read at a glance: emission nebulae one color and planetary nebulae a similar (related) hue, galaxies their own color, clusters their own color. M42 (OpenNGC type Cl+N) must get the nebula color, not the cluster color. Regenerate examples/skymap.html + skymap.png and the README screenshot afterward. Consider documenting the chosen palette as a suggested type-color scheme in the README (config remains per-object color; no new engine feature required).

comments.author: Devon Jones
comments.text: Scope addition: all objects now have finished/ renders. Swap the example heroes to the processed versions: M1/finished/M1_64.jpg, M31/finished/M31_28.jpg, M42/finished/M42_72.jpg, M45/finished/M45_33.jpg (replacing the seestar-stack placeholders in examples/images/), keeping the existing M27/M51/Sh2-142 finished picks. Refresh examples/images/, skymap.html, skymap.png, README screenshot.

title: Sky map: image thumbnails at deep zoom and on marker hover
description: Two related displays of an object's photo on the chart itself. (1) Deep zoom: when a disc is zoomed past a threshold (e.g. --z >= ~4), markers whose object has an image show a small thumbnail beside the reticle (SVG <image>, counter-scaled like labels so it keeps screen size); below the threshold it hides. (2) Hover: pointing at a marker (and maybe a legend card) shows a floating thumbnail tooltip at any zoom level, positioned to avoid the cursor and chart edge. Implementation notes: build-time downscaled thumbnails (~96-128px, JPEG/WebP) embedded as data URIs to keep the page self-contained — the full-size image stays a path/URL for the lightbox. Needs Pillow at build time; make thumbnails opt-out (thumbnails: true default when Pillow is present, warn-and-skip otherwise, or a [thumbs] extra). Watch page-weight on 100+ object charts (~5-15KB per thumb); cap or config. Interacts with uranometria-5 (lightbox/zoom interaction) and uranometria-4 (annotation overlay).

title: Chart: drag-to-pan selects/highlights all map text
description: Dragging on a chart disc to pan visually selects the SVG text (constellation names, marker labels, hour labels) — the browser treats the drag as a text selection. Fix: user-select:none (+ -webkit-user-select) on the chart svg (or .charts), and/or preventDefault on pointerdown before starting the pan so no selection begins; clear any existing selection when a pan starts. Verify labels are still real text for accessibility (selection off is a display choice, not outlined text).

title: Chart: wheel at max zoom bleeds into page/column scroll
description: Repro: zoom fully in with the mouse wheel over a disc; once the zoom clamps at max, continued wheeling starts scrolling the surrounding column/page instead of being swallowed. Expected: wheel events over the chart are always consumed (preventDefault), even when the zoom is already at its clamp in that direction. Investigate: the wheel handler calls preventDefault unconditionally, so find where the leak is — possibly events landing on the zoomhint/footer/hemi-toggle as the pointer drifts off the svg while the disc shrinks visually, or listener passivity on some path. Fix and verify at both clamp ends (max zoom-in and full zoom-out) on single- and dual-hemisphere charts.
